item.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

item.title: Almost half of U.S. singles feel negatively about AI in dating, Match says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: About 47% of singles look negatively at the use of AI in dating -- but, many dating app users are open to AI helping with profile punch-ups and conversation starters.

item.title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy has said this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.

item.title: AI data centers just got a government-mandated fast lane to the grid
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: FERC told grid operators to give data centers a fast lane for interconnections, but it failed to address electricity supply shortages.

item.title: The smartphone era created an attention crisis. Slowtech is fixing it
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: “People just really want to take back control of their time, their lives, their attention... They’re down for whatever helps them do that.”

item.title: ‘Queer Eye’s’ life coach Karamo Brown launches Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Karamo Brown, famous for his pep talks on Netflix’s “Queer Eye,” has jumped into the wellness and AI space with his new app, Kē. After spending a year and a half focusing on his own journey—from fitness and nutrition to meditation, sobriety, relationships, and personal growth—Brown wants to help others do the same.  Kē offers […]

item.title: General Intuition in talks to raise $300M at around $2B valuation
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The startup trains embodied AI and world models using Medal’s dataset of 2 billion videos per year from 10 million monthly active users.

item.title: A tech worker-backed PAC is bringing a $5M knife to Big Tech’s $100M gunfight
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Guardrails positions itself as a populist political movement that runs on small donations from people in the trenches of the AI boom.

item.title: Pixi’s new iOS app turns text messages into interactive AR experiences
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Forget stickers, GIFs, and emoji reactions. Pixi is betting that the next evolution of messaging is interactive augmented reality (AR).

item.title: How to turn off AI in your Google Docs
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Here's what you need to do to get those pesky "write with Gemini" pop-ups to go away.

item.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

item.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut hasn't exactly done wonders for the company's stock.

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses for parts of their org, and Meta killed its internal leaderboard.  This tension between […]

item.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.

item.title: Anthropic becomes first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.

item.title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Social media feeds are becoming more customizable as platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok introduce tools that let users directly influence the algorithms powering their recommendations.

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck on AI IPOs, personal agents, and the ROI reckoning
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan, Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses for parts of their org, and Meta killed its internal leaderboard.  This tension between […]

item.title: World model maker Odyssey nabs $1.45B valuation backed by Amazon and other big names
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: World models are the next big thing in AI beyond LLMs and, with this round, Odyssey has cemented itself as one of the startups to watch.

item.title: Only 16 percent of Americans think AI will have a positive impact on society, a new study shows
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Although Wall Street loves AI, every day Americans are significantly less optimistic about the industry, a new report from Pew Research shows.

item.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.
